description_long: A rectangular terracotta planter box is densely filled with a variety of flowering plants. The flowers are predominantly orange and yellow, with daisy-like blooms and a few taller, tulip-shaped blossoms rising above the rest. The foliage is lush and green, with leaves of varying shapes and sizes, creating a layered and naturalistic appearance. The planter itself has a weathered, earthy finish, suggesting it is made of clay or ceramic. Some stems and leaves extend slightly beyond the edges of the planter, adding to the organic, slightly untamed look. This arrangement would be commonly found on urban sidewalks, patios, or in public parks as decorative greenery.
description: A weathered terracotta planter box filled with dense, orange and yellow daisy-like and tulip-shaped flowers, surrounded by lush green foliage.
description_view_0: Front view shows the rectangular planter with a dense cluster of orange and yellow flowers and green foliage, with taller blooms rising above the rest.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the depth of the planter and the vertical arrangement of the tallest flowers, with foliage appearing more compact.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the planter, with flowers and leaves spilling slightly over the edge, and the tallest blooms visible at the center.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows the planter's width and the layered arrangement of flowers and leaves, with some stems leaning outward.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial pattern, with daisy-like flowers displaying multiple layers of overlapping petals radiating from a central disk, and tulip-shaped flowers exhibiting a whorled arrangement with some overlapping at the base.
